payload.instructions:
# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization
- Source lives in `src` with layered domains: `domain` (pure entities/types), `service` (IO such as HTTP/local storage), `state` (business logic and React-query hooks), `ui` (presentational components), and `pages` (composition only). Each feature follows a modlet pattern: import from its `index.ts` to keep boundaries clean.
- Tests sit beside code as `*.test.ts(x)` plus shared helpers in `test/`; end-to-end and visual checks live under `tests/` and `playwright` assets.
- Static assets and mocks live in `public/`. Built bundles land in `dist/`. Storybook stories belong next to components inside `ui`.

## Build, Test, and Development Commands
- `yarn install` then `yarn create-cert` once to generate HTTPS certs via mkcert; start the app with `yarn dev` (`https://anedot.me:3001`).
- `yarn build` creates a production bundle; `yarn serve` or `yarn preview` runs the built output locally. Use `yarn bundle-analyze` for a visual bundle report.
- `yarn storybook` launches component docs at `http://localhost:6006`; `yarn build-storybook` produces a static build.
- `yarn lint` (or `yarn lint:fix`) runs Biome. Dependency graph checks: `yarn deps` or `yarn deps:svg`.

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
- TypeScript + React 17 with Chakra UI. Components and files use `PascalCase`; hooks use `use*`; utility modules prefer `camelCase`. Keep exports funneled through each feature’s `index.ts`.
- Biome enforces formatting (2-space indent) and lint rules (`biome.json`). Respect domain/service/state/ui boundaries; pages should avoid styling and only compose UI.
- Volta pins Node 20.16.0; prefer Yarn for scripts. Avoid committing generated certs or environment files.

## Testing Guidelines
- `yarn test` runs Jest and Vitest suites; CI uses `yarn test:ci` (coverage enforced in Jest). Use Testing Library for React units and keep mocks close to the feature.
- `yarn test:playwright` covers user flows; place scenarios in `tests/` with clear, behavior-driven names.
- When adding components, include Storybook stories and at least one unit or integration test. Prefer deterministic data over networked mocks; use fixtures or state-layer mocks instead.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
- Use Commitizen (`npx cz`) for conventional commit messages; Husky runs lint-staged on commit and TypeScript checks on push.
- Before opening a PR: ensure `yarn lint` and `yarn test` pass, add coverage for new behavior, and update stories/docs when UI changes.
- PR descriptions should summarize intent, link issues/tickets, and include screenshots or GIFs for UI-affecting work. Call out any configuration changes (env vars, scripts) explicitly.

## Security & Configuration Tips
- Create `.env.local` (ask a teammate for values) for Vite; never commit secrets. HTTPS dev requires mkcert (`yarn create-cert` writes to `.cert/`).
- Rollbar and Graylog are used in non-local environments—avoid logging sensitive data and scrub PII in new instrumentation.

# AGENTS.md instructions for /Users/duhl/git/anedot-frontend

<INSTRUCTIONS>
# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization
- Source lives in `src` with layered domains: `domain` (pure entities/types), `service` (IO such as HTTP/local storage), `state` (business logic and React-query hooks), `ui` (presentational components), and `pages` (composition only). Each feature follows a modlet pattern: import from its `index.ts` to keep boundaries clean.
- Tests sit beside code as `*.test.ts(x)` plus shared helpers in `test/`; end-to-end and visual checks live under `tests/` and `playwright` assets.
- Static assets and mocks live in `public/`. Built bundles land in `dist/`. Storybook stories belong next to components inside `ui`.

## Build, Test, and Development Commands
- `yarn install` then `yarn create-cert` once to generate HTTPS certs via mkcert; start the app with `yarn dev` (`https://anedot.me:3001`).
- `yarn build` creates a production bundle; `yarn serve` or `yarn preview` runs the built output locally. Use `yarn bundle-analyze` for a visual bundle report.
- `yarn storybook` launches component docs at `http://localhost:6006`; `yarn build-storybook` produces a static build.
- `yarn lint` (or `yarn lint:fix`) runs Biome. Dependency graph checks: `yarn deps` or `yarn deps:svg`.

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
- TypeScript + React 17 with Chakra UI. Components and files use `PascalCase`; hooks use `use*`; utility modules prefer `camelCase`. Keep exports funneled through each feature’s `index.ts`.
- Biome enforces formatting (2-space indent) and lint rules (`biome.json`). Respect domain/service/state/ui boundaries; pages should avoid styling and only compose UI.
- Volta pins Node 20.16.0; prefer Yarn for scripts. Avoid committing generated certs or environment files.

## Testing Guidelines
- `yarn test` runs Jest and Vitest suites; CI uses `yarn test:ci` (coverage enforced in Jest). Use Testing Library for React units and keep mocks close to the feature.
- `yarn test:playwright` covers user flows; place scenarios in `tests/` with clear, behavior-driven names.
- When adding components, include Storybook stories and at least one unit or integration test. Prefer deterministic data over networked mocks; use fixtures or state-layer mocks instead.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
- Use Commitizen (`npx cz`) for conventional commit messages; Husky runs lint-staged on commit and TypeScript checks on push.
- Before opening a PR: ensure `yarn lint` and `yarn test` pass, add coverage for new behavior, and update stories/docs when UI changes.
- PR descriptions should summarize intent, link issues/tickets, and include screenshots or GIFs for UI-affecting work. Call out any configuration changes (env vars, scripts) explicitly.

## Security & Configuration Tips
- Create `.env.local` (ask a teammate for values) for Vite; never commit secrets. HTTPS dev requires mkcert (`yarn create-cert` writes to `.cert/`).
- Rollbar and Graylog are used in non-local environments—avoid logging sensitive data and scrub PII in new instrumentation.

</INSTRUCTIONS>

payload.message: Updated `scripts/replace-i18n.ts` to swap `replaceAll` for a global regex via `String.replace`, keeping behavior while avoiding newer API usage.
